</imports>

<findings>

[observation] [info] High cohesion (1) — files are tightly interconnected
[observation] [info] No entry points are declared for this zone despite it being the foundation tier imported by hench and web; adding explicit entry point declarations to tooling configuration would make the public API surface more auditable.
[observation] [info] Perfect isolation (cohesion 1.0, coupling 0.0) across 41 files is the strongest quality signal in the monorepo — this zone exemplifies correct foundation-tier design and serves as a reference for other packages.
[observation] [info] The provider abstraction (api-provider, cli-provider, codex-cli-provider, create-client) enables vendor substitution without caller changes; ensure new vendor additions follow the existing provider interface to keep hench's llm-gateway.ts as the sole integration point.
[pattern] [info] The foundation tier boundary (llm-foundation) is the best-enforced tier boundary in the monorepo: only two gateways (hench/src/prd/llm-gateway.ts and web/src/server/domain-gateway.ts) cross it, and no leaf file in any other zone imports directly from llm-client — this is the only tier where the gateway contract has zero known violations.
[suggestion] [info] llm-foundation/package.json has a './dist/*' wildcard export that makes all dist files accessible to intra-monorepo consumers, bypassing the './public.js' API boundary. Adding entry point declarations to sourcevision zone configuration for the provider interface files would create a second enforcement layer that flags direct internal file imports without requiring changes to the package.json exports.

</findings>

<insights>

- High cohesion (1) — files are tightly interconnected
- Perfect cohesion (1.0) and zero outward coupling confirm this zone is a true foundation layer with no upward dependencies — the architecture constraint is working
- At 41 files with multiple provider implementations (api-provider, cli-provider, codex-cli-provider), this zone is the canonical extension point for adding new LLM vendors without touching hench or web
- The zero-coupling property must be actively protected: any addition that imports from rex, sourcevision, hench, or web would break the foundation tier contract
- Perfect isolation (cohesion 1.0, coupling 0.0) across 41 files is the strongest quality signal in the monorepo — this zone exemplifies correct foundation-tier design and serves as a reference for other packages.
- The provider abstraction (api-provider, cli-provider, codex-cli-provider, create-client) enables vendor substitution without caller changes; ensure new vendor additions follow the existing provider interface to keep hench's llm-gateway.ts as the sole integration point.
- No entry points are declared for this zone despite it being the foundation tier imported by hench and web; adding explicit entry point declarations to tooling configuration would make the public API surface more auditable.
- llm-foundation is the only zone that achieves perfect isolation (cohesion 1.0, coupling 0.0) while being actively imported by two separate packages (hench via llm-gateway.ts, web via domain-gateway.ts) — the zero-coupling metric is possible only because all consumers mediate access through gateways, making this the sole confirmed case in the monorepo where the gateway pattern fully decouples a heavily-used foundation from its consumers.
- The foundation tier boundary (llm-foundation) is the best-enforced tier boundary in the monorepo: only two gateways (hench/src/prd/llm-gateway.ts and web/src/server/domain-gateway.ts) cross it, and no leaf file in any other zone imports directly from llm-client — this is the only tier where the gateway contract has zero known violations.
- llm-foundation exposes a package-level API boundary through package.json exports ('./': './dist/public.js') but sourcevision zone analysis records zero declared entry points. The package.json export constrains external package consumers but does NOT constrain intra-monorepo consumers, who can import directly from './dist/internal-file.js' via the './dist/*' escape-hatch export without any tooling warning. The zone-level entry point declarations would close this intra-monorepo gap that package.json exports cannot address.
- The './dist/*' wildcard export in llm-client/package.json is documented in CLAUDE.md as an 'intentional escape hatch' but combined with zero declared entry points in zone analysis, it means every file in llm-foundation is implicitly public API — there is no machine-checkable distinction between stable public interface and internal implementation detail anywhere in the package boundary definition.
- llm-foundation/package.json has a './dist/*' wildcard export that makes all dist files accessible to intra-monorepo consumers, bypassing the './public.js' API boundary. Adding entry point declarations to sourcevision zone configuration for the provider interface files would create a second enforcement layer that flags direct internal file imports without requiring changes to the package.json exports.
- [call graph] 494 internal calls, 0 outgoing, 0 incoming (cohesion: 1, coupling: 0)

</insights>
